definition: Loading a scene without unloading the current one — both scenes exist simultaneously. Used for persistent managers, streaming open worlds, or layered level design.
A MonoBehaviour component that drives animation playback on a GameObject. Requires an Animator Controller asset and plays clips according to that state machine's logic.

A state machine asset (created via right-click → Create → Animator Controller) that defines animation states, transitions between them, and parameters (Bool, Float, Int, Trigger) that scripts can set to drive transitions.
A recorded timeline asset containing keyframe data — changes to position, rotation, scale, component properties — over time. The raw material that Animator Controller states play.
A marker placed on an Animation Clip timeline that calls a named method on scripts attached to the same GameObject when the playhead passes that frame.
An asset holding imported audio data (.wav, .mp3, .ogg). Configured with Load Type (Compressed, Streaming, Decompress on Load) and Channels in the Inspector.
A component (typically on the Main Camera) that acts as the 'ear' of the scene. Exactly one should exist — all 3D AudioSources are spatialized relative to its position and orientation.
A component that plays AudioClips in the scene. Key properties: Clip, Volume, Pitch, Loop, Play On Awake, Spatial Blend, and Output (to an Audio Mixer Group).
A skeleton mapping definition for a humanoid or generic rig. Humanoid Avatars allow Mecanim to retarget animations from one character model to another with a different bone hierarchy.
Pre-computed lighting stored in Lightmap textures on static geometry. Has zero runtime cost but cannot change dynamically. Objects must be marked Static → Contribute GI to be included.
An Animator Controller state that smoothly blends between multiple Animation Clips based on one or two float parameters. The standard approach for locomotion (idle/walk/run blending).
The editor dialog (File → Build Settings) where you specify scenes to include in the build, select the target platform, and configure platform-specific settings before exporting the game.
The root container for all Unity UI elements. Every Text, Image, Button, and Slider must be a child of a Canvas. Manages render mode (Overlay, Screen Space, World Space) and UI scaling.
A component on Canvas that controls how UI scales across different resolutions. Scale With Screen Size mode lets you define a reference resolution and scales the entire Canvas to match.
A component providing collision-based movement without a Rigidbody. Uses controller.Move(velocity) for deterministic character control with built-in step offset and slope limit handling.
A component that defines the physical shape of a GameObject for physics collision detection. Variants: BoxCollider, SphereCollider, CapsuleCollider, MeshCollider. Can be a Trigger.
Merges multiple 2D colliders (typically from a Tilemap Collider 2D) into a single optimized physics shape, dramatically reducing the number of physics contacts the engine must process.
A method (returning IEnumerator) that can pause execution mid-frame using yield return and resume later. Runs on the main thread but doesn't block it. Used for timed sequences and asynchronous flows.
A property on the Camera component (LayerMask) that controls which layers the camera renders. Layers not in the mask are invisible to that camera.
Renders a line in the Scene view (not visible in Game view or builds) for a duration. Used to visualize raycasts, movement vectors, and debug spatial logic without adding visible objects.
A static method that marks a GameObject to persist when a new scene is loaded. Used for managers (AudioManager, GameManager) that must survive scene transitions.
A required Unity scene object that processes input events (clicks, touch, keyboard navigation) for all Canvas UI elements. Without it, UI interaction is completely non-functional.
A MonoBehaviour lifecycle method called at a fixed timestep (default 50×/second) independent of frame rate. All Rigidbody force application should happen here for stable, deterministic physics.

An enum used with Rigidbody.AddForce to specify how force is applied: Force (continuous, mass-dependent), Impulse (instant, mass-dependent), Acceleration (continuous, ignores mass), VelocityChange (instant, ignores mass).
The fundamental object type in Unity — a container for Components. A GameObject itself has no behavior; it only acts through the components attached to it. Every object in a scene is a GameObject.
A method available on all MonoBehaviours and GameObjects to retrieve a component of type T attached to the same GameObject. Returns null if the component doesn't exist. Cache results in Awake.
Simulation of indirect lighting — light that has bounced off surfaces to illuminate other surfaces. Can be Baked (pre-computed into lightmaps) or Realtime (updated dynamically using Enlighten).
The Unity editor panel listing every GameObject in the open scene as a collapsible parent-child tree. Drag objects onto others to parent them. Parented objects inherit transform changes.
The Unity editor panel displaying all components and properties of the selected GameObject or asset. Used to configure component values, add/remove components, and view script fields.
A static Unity method that creates a copy of a prefab or existing GameObject at a specified position and rotation. Returns the new instance as a UnityEngine.Object (cast to use it).

A Rigidbody property that removes the object from physics simulation forces (gravity, collisions won't move it) while keeping its collider active. Kinematic Rigidbodies are moved by script via MovePosition/MoveRotation.
A Collider property that makes the collider non-physical — objects pass through it, but OnTriggerEnter/Stay/Exit callbacks still fire. Used for detection zones, pickups, and area effects.
A MonoBehaviour lifecycle method called every frame after all Update() calls have completed. Ideal for camera follow logic to react to final player positions rather than mid-frame positions.
An integer index (0-31) assigned to GameObjects. Used by Camera Culling Masks to filter rendering and by the Physics Layer Collision Matrix to control which objects can collide.
Found in Project Settings → Physics. A grid defining which physics layers can interact with each other. Use it to prevent bullets from colliding with the player who fired them, etc.
A pre-computed texture baked onto static geometry storing lighting information (diffuse, shadows, GI bounces). Applied by Unity's lighting system to avoid recalculating lighting at runtime.
Sample points placed in the scene that capture baked indirect lighting for dynamic objects (characters, moving props) that can't use lightmaps. Stored as spherical harmonics coefficients.
An asset pairing a Shader with property values (textures, colors, metallic, smoothness). Applied to MeshRenderer components to define how a surface appears when rendered.
Unity's modern animation system — encompasses Animator Controller state machines, Blend Trees, humanoid Avatar retargeting, IK solving, and Animation Layers. Replaced the legacy Animation component.
The base class for Unity scripts. Inheriting from MonoBehaviour gives access to lifecycle methods (Awake, Start, Update, etc.), the component system (GetComponent), and Unity's serialization system.
Unity's built-in package management window (Window → Package Manager). Used to install, update, enable, and remove Unity Registry packages, Asset Store purchases, and custom Git/local packages.
Casts an invisible ray from a point in a direction and returns true if it intersects a collider. Fills a RaycastHit struct with hit point, normal, distance, and collider reference.
A simple key-value storage system (int, float, string) that persists data between game sessions. Suitable for settings and small data. Not suitable for secure save data — values are user-editable.

An asset storing a fully configured GameObject (components, children, values) as a reusable template. Instances in scenes link to the Prefab; changes to the Prefab propagate to all instances unless overridden.

A Prefab that inherits from a parent Prefab. Variants can override individual property values while inheriting all other properties from the original, enabling specialized versions of shared templates.
Unity's performance analysis tool (Window → Analysis → Profiler) that records per-frame CPU, GPU, memory, rendering, physics, and audio data to identify performance bottlenecks.
A struct populated by Physics.Raycast when a hit occurs. Contains: point (world position of hit), normal (surface direction), distance (from origin), collider, transform, and rigidbody references.
A component that enables physics simulation on a 3D GameObject — gravity, velocity, forces, and collision responses controlled by PhysX. Without it, a Collider is static and unaffected by forces.
A Transform variant used by all Unity UI elements. Adds Width/Height, Anchors, Pivot, and offset properties to control how UI elements position and scale relative to their parent Canvas or panel.
A data container asset that lives in the Project window, independent of scenes. Used to store shared data (item stats, enemy configs) without needing a scene GameObject. Supports asset references.
A C# attribute that exposes a private field in the Unity Inspector without making it public. Preferred over public fields for maintaining encapsulation while allowing designer-editable values.
A GPU program that calculates the final rendered color of every pixel on a surface. Defines what material properties are exposed (textures, colors, metallic) and how they interact with light.
A Material applied as the scene's background environment — wraps the camera's view with a cube map or procedural sky. Also influences ambient lighting and reflections. Assigned in Lighting settings.
Named layers that control the overall draw order for 2D renderers (Sprite Renderer, Tilemap Renderer). Sorting Layers take priority over Order in Layer when comparing across different layers.
An asset that packs multiple sprites into a single texture. Reduces draw calls because all sprites from the atlas can be rendered in one GPU batch instead of separate calls per sprite.
A string label assigned to a GameObject in the Inspector. Used for identification in scripts: gameObject.CompareTag("Enemy") or GameObject.FindWithTag("Player"). Tags are defined in Project Settings.

Unity's recommended text rendering solution using signed distance field technology for sharp text at any scale. Supports rich text formatting, advanced styling, and better performance than legacy Text.
The time elapsed in seconds between the current and previous frame. Multiply all movement, rotation, and other per-frame values by this to achieve frame-rate independent behavior.
A Unity 2D system for efficiently painting tile-based levels on a grid using Tile assets and the Tile Palette window. Works with Tilemap Renderer, Tilemap Collider 2D, and Rule Tiles.
The core component on every GameObject storing its position, rotation, and scale. Also manages parent-child relationships. Cannot be removed. A child's transform values are relative to its parent.
